title: lithocarpus brochidodromus
intro: Lithocarpus brochidodromus, also known as the Tanbark Oak, is a species of evergreen tree native to Asia, belonging to the beech family Fagaceae. Its bark is traditionally used in herbal medicine, and it contains compounds with anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may be used to treat various health issues. The health benefits of Lithocarpus brochidodromus include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate digestive problems, and support immune function. In traditional herbal preparations, the bark can be dried, powdered, and made into teas, infusions, or capsules to harness its medicinal properties.
